item.title: US Jobs Up 115,000, First Back-to-Back Gain in Nearly a Year
item.description: US employers added to payrolls for a second month in April, marking the first back-to-back advance in nearly a year, and the unemployment rate held steady.

item.title: Canada Employment Fell by 17,700, Jobless Rate Climbs to 6.9%
item.description: The Canadian economy shed 17,700 jobs in April while more people looked for work, pushing the unemployment rate up to 6.9%.

item.title: Argentina Soyoil Trades at Decade Low to US on Harvest, Biofuels
item.description: Soybean oil from Argentina, the world’s biggest exporter, is trading at the cheapest in at least a decade relative to rival supplies out of the US, as the harvest on the Pampas crop belt hits full stride and after Washington increased biofuel blending mandates.

item.title: Chile Hit by Biggest Consumer Price Jump Since 2022 on Fuel Hike
item.description: Chile’s consumer prices in April posted the biggest monthly jump since 2022, reflecting the brunt of of President José Antonio Kast’s decision to let fuel costs surge after the onset of the Middle East war.

item.title: Elliott Loses Fight Over $8.7 Billion Buyout of China’s 58.com
item.description: Elliott Investment Management, T. Rowe Price Group Inc. and other money managers failed to persuade a Cayman Islands judge to award more than the deal price in the $8.7 billion take-private of China’s largest online classified-ad marketplace six years ago.

item.title: Economists Cast Doubt on Milei’s Green Shoots in Weary Argentina
item.description: President Javier Milei and leading experts are at odds over whether Argentina’s economy has recovered from a recent wobble, highlighting the challenges involved in gauging whether the reformer’s shock therapy policies are working as intended.

item.title: Record French Solar Power Output Pushes Prices Below Zero
item.description: French solar output surged to a record on Friday, exceeding grid operator forecasts and pushing power prices below zero.

item.title: Fannie, Freddie’s Latest Bull Says Traders Underpricing IPO Odds
item.description: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac investors are underestimating the chances of a public market re-entry from the mortgage giants after a lull in chatter around the names, according to Mizuho’s Dan Dolev.

item.title: Southeast Asia Boosts Maritime Ties to Avoid Hormuz-Like Crisis
item.description: Southeast Asian nations agreed to boost cooperation and create a center for maritime issues, which the Philippines said could help avert a situation in the South China Sea similar to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Can Companies Push Price This Time?
item.description: Maybe it’s tougher.

item.title: Trump OKs US Oil Pipe, Undercutting Canadian Plan Backed by Carney
item.description: Ottawa wants to weaken its dependence on the US, but Washington has other ideas.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 8, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders await Iran’s response to a US-proposed deal to end the war.

item.title: Marcos Says Asean Leaders Seek Improvement in Myanmar Relations
item.description: Normalizing relations with conflict-torn Myanmar was a “big subject” of discussions among regional leaders gathering this week in the Philippines, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said Friday.

item.title: US Futures Rise as Chip Stocks Rally, Iran Ceasefire Holds
item.description: Stock futures climbed on Friday as top semiconductor stocks gained ahead of a payrolls report that’s expected to show a modest increase in hiring. Oil held steady as the Iran ceasefire appeared intact even with overnight clashes near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: PE Firms Tap Europe’s Junk Market for Dividends as Exits Stall
item.description: Private equity firms are once again tapping the European junk debt market to pay themselves dividends as market volatility fueled by the Iran war and AI anxiety limits their ability to cash out.

item.title: ‘Peak Euphoria’ Seen as Small Companies Jump on the AI Bandwagon
item.description: A tiny pharmaceutical company in danger of being delisted from the Nasdaq stock exchange went from celebrating new patents for cancer treatments to re-branding as an AI company in the period of two months. The move rescued its share price in the process, albeit briefly.

item.title: Hunt for AI Optical Winners Drives Rally in Two Swiss Stocks
item.description: European investors chasing AI winners have turned to Switzerland, sending shares of two optical component makers sharply higher this week.

item.title: HSBC Conducted ‘Thorough’ Review of Fraud Provision, Chair Says
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc has carried out a “thorough review” of a $400 million fraud-related provision in its latest earnings and is updating its risk appetite, according to Chairman Brendan Nelson.

item.title: Analysis of US Jobs Report for April
item.description: Jobseekers speak with recruiters during a job fair in Fletcher, North Carolina. Photographer: Allison Joyce/Bloomberg

item.title: AI Startup Monk Offers a Year’s Rent for One Good Recruiting Tip
item.description: The company is making a creative pitch to find engineers in a highly competitive market

item.title: Salmon Farms on Land Take Aim at a $19 Billion Industry
item.description: They could upend the fishing sector—but only if companies can bring the costs down first.

item.title: New Oil Shock Prompts Asian Governments to Intervene in Markets
item.description: Governments across Asia are intervening into fossil fuel markets to try and smooth over disruptions.

item.title: Box Makers Struggle to Pass on War Costs as Demand Stays Weak
item.description: Packaging producers can’t catch a break as the conflict in the Middle East deals the latest blow to the industry’s protracted downturn.

item.title: Black Eyed Peas Co-Founder will.i.am Is Also an AI Evangelist
item.description: Black Eyed Peas co-founder will.i.am has a message for young people worried that artificial intelligence will make it hard for them to get jobs: embrace the technology, he says, and make it work for you.

item.title: Uber-Backed Electric Scooter Rental Firm Lime Files for IPO
item.description: Electric bike and scooter rental startup Lime filed for an initial public offering, showing growing revenue and an expanding user base.

item.title: Treasuries Gain Ahead of US Payrolls With Jobs Growth Expected
item.description: Treasuries gained ahead of US employment data that looks set to cement the Federal Reserve’s cautious approach to changing policy.

item.title: DoubleLine’s Jeffrey Gundlach Is Preparing for an Extreme Debt Scenario
item.description: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach thinks the US government might restructure its debt.

item.title: Trump Wants to Make H-1B Workers More Expensive for US Employers
item.description: An entry‑level software engineer in San Francisco would need to be paid $162,000 a year to qualify for an H‑1B visa under a Trump administration proposal — almost 30% more than today. In Dallas, the minimum would jump by a similar rate to $113,000 and in New York to $132,000.

item.title: Intesa Sanpaolo Beats on Profit as Trading Revenue Surges
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA‘s first-quarter results came in better than expected on the back of a jump in trading revenue.

item.title: Japan Likely Refrained From Currency Intervention After Holidays
item.description: Japanese authorities likely refrained from intervening in the currency market on Thursday, the first business day of this week, after multiple apparent actions during the long holiday weekend, according to a Bloomberg analysis of central bank accounts.
